sourceMovie.title: Layer Cake
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Layer Cake for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Matthew Vaughn keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jackie Brown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Layer Cake clicked for you, Jackie Brown (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Layer Cake?
faq.answer: Try Jackie Brown, A Walk Among the Tombstones, Payback—each shares DNA with Layer Cake while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Layer Cake?
faq.answer: Layer Cake (2004) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Layer Cake worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
